- **VS Code SCM parent repo disappears**: A writer is still targeting `.specstory/logs/` and creating files that expose the nested `.git` — audit all writers against `lsl-paths.js` constants
- **`system.log` missing from `history/logs/`**: `OperationalLogger.js` has not been updated or the service has not restarted — verify the path in `OperationalLogger.js` and restart the container/service

entities.name: Private History Repo — Bootstrap and Migration
observations:
## Purpose
Prevents Claude session transcripts (chat logs) from leaking into public GitHub repositories by storing `.specstory/history/` in a dedicated private `<project>-history` repo on GHE for every project managed by `coding/bin/coding`.

## Architecture
- **Bootstrap function**: `ensure_private_history_repo` in `scripts/agent-common-setup.sh`
  - Derives default history remote URL by appending `-history` to the outer repo's remote name
  - Prompts interactively on first run with skip/accept/override options; writes a skip marker if declined
  - Silently skips repos where `.specstory/history/.git` already exists
  - Supports `LSL_HISTORY_AUTO` and `LSL_HISTORY_REMOTE_TEMPLATE` env vars for CI/non-interactive contexts
  - Falls back to `gh`-based hints on push failure
- **Migration script**: `scripts/migrate-history-to-private.sh`
  - Uses `git-filter-repo` to purge `.specstory/history/` from public repo history
  - Includes preflight checks (tooling availability, clean working tree)
  - Pushes purged history to corresponding GHE private repo (`<ORG>.ghe.com/<USER>/<REPO>-history`)
  - Fixed cosmetic HTTPS-URL parser bug that produced malformed URLs (extra slashes)
- **Canonical private remote**: `Frank-Woernle/coding-history` on GHE for the `coding` project; confirmed as sole remote origin for `.specstory/history`

## Key Files
- `scripts/agent-common-setup.sh` — contains `ensure_private_history_repo` function
- `scripts/migrate-history-to-private.sh` — bulk migration script for public repos leaking history

## Usage
`ensure_private_history_repo` runs automatically when `coding/bin/coding` initialises a project. For existing public repos with leaked history, run `migrate-history-to-private.sh` per-repo after confirming `git-filter-repo` is available.

## Troubleshooting
- **Bootstrap silently skipped**: A skip marker exists from a prior declined prompt — remove the marker file to re-trigger the prompt
- **Malformed GHE remote URL**: URL parser bug producing `ghe.com///...` — verify the HTTPS-URL parsing logic in `migrate-history-to-private.sh`
- **Corporate remote not force-pushed after purge**: Some repos have secondary remotes (e.g., `cc-github.bmwgroup.net`) that must be force-pushed separately — `migrate-history-to-private.sh` only handles the primary remote
- **`git-filter-repo` not found**: Install via `pip install git-filter-repo`; the script's preflight check will block execution if missing

entities.name: Docker-Only Architecture — Native Mode Removal
observations:
## Purpose
Simplifies the codebase by removing dual native/Docker-mode code paths that were never exercised in production, reducing cognitive overhead and eliminating dead signal handlers and feature flags across the service layer.

## Architecture
Removed across all service scripts:
- `isDockerMode` / `isTransitionLocked` / `monitoringPaused` flags and their conditional branches
- Native-mode fallback code paths
- `SIGUSR` signal handlers used for mode transitions
- `docker-mode-transition.js` (deleted entirely — no longer imported)

**Affected scripts (Phase C)**: `post-session-logger.js`, `global-process-supervisor.js`, `health-remediation-actions.js`, `combined-status-line.js`, `start-services-robust.js`, `health-verifier.js`, `statusline-health-monitor.js`, `global-service-coordinator.js`

**Documentation (Phase D)**: Deleted obsolete native-mode/transition files (`docker-mode-transition.md`, `docker-mode-architecture.puml`, `docker-health-detection.puml`, associated PNGs); updated 14 markdown files and 4 PUML diagrams across `docs/` and `docs-content/` to reflect docker-only architecture.

## Key Files
- `scripts/global-process-supervisor.js` — supervisor; native-mode branches removed
- `scripts/global-service-coordinator.js` — coordinator; transition logic removed
- `scripts/combined-status-line.js` — statusline; provider display and native fallbacks removed
- `docs/` and `docs-content/` — updated to docker-only architecture; obsolete PUML/PNG files deleted

## Usage
All service scripts now assume Docker runtime unconditionally. No mode-detection logic should be re-introduced.

## Troubleshooting
- **Script references `isDockerMode`**: The variable no longer exists — the calling code is stale and must be updated to remove the conditional
- **`docker-mode-transition.js` import error**: File has been deleted; remove the import from any script still referencing it
- **SIGUSR signal handler missing**: Intentionally removed; if a script relied on SIGUSR for mode switching, that functionality no longer exists

entities.name: Coding-Services Container — Idle CPU Optimisation
observations:
## Purpose
Reduces `coding-services` container CPU usage from ~8-30% idle to ~1-2% by eliminating unnecessary high-frequency polling and adding stat-gated file reads.

## Architecture
Four targeted fixes:

1. **`scripts/global-process-supervisor.js`**: DEBUG file-write gate — skips log writes unless DEBUG level is active (~9 MB/day write reduction)

2. **`scripts/health-prompt-hook.js`**: cwd-robust LSL check — eliminates false alarms from path-resolution failures

3. **`integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/store/middleware/healthRefreshMiddleware.ts`**: adaptive polling with visibility gate — cuts frontend polling rate ~10× when tab is hidden or idle

4. **`integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js`** (primary fix):
   - SSE handler rewritten to stat-gate the 618 KB JSON read on `mtimeMs + size` — only reads file when content has changed
   - Adaptive intervals: 100 ms when active clients connected, 1 s when idle
   - Removed dead `watcher` and `debounceTimer` declarations
   - Eliminated a 100 ms `setInterval` at line ~1031 that fired unconditionally 10×/second regardless of client connections

**Remaining investigation**: intermittent 7-8% spikes on a ~12-14 s cadence from `code-graph-rag` Python SSE server identified as next optimisation target.

## Key Files
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — SSE handler, stat-gate, adaptive intervals
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/store/middleware/healthRefreshMiddleware.ts` — frontend adaptive polling
- `scripts/global-process-supervisor.js` — DEBUG write gate
- `scripts/health-prompt-hook.js` — cwd-robust LSL check

## Usage
Fixes are passive — no configuration required. Use `docker stats` + `top -bn2` (delta-based per-PID) to profile CPU attribution inside the container.

## Troubleshooting
- **Idle CPU spikes to 7-8% on ~12-14 s cadence**: Likely `code-graph-rag` Python SSE server (PID 132 observed at 84% in a single tick) — not yet fixed; profile with `top -bn2` inside container to confirm
- **SSE handler CPU high with no connected clients**: Verify the adaptive interval logic in `server.js` is active and the stat-gate compares `mtimeMs + size` before reading the 618 KB JSON
- **Frontend polling too frequent**: Confirm `healthRefreshMiddleware.ts` visibility gate is active; browser tab visibility API must be supported

entities.name: Health Coordinator — Phase 33 Consolidated Health Monitoring
observations:
## Purpose
Consolidates all service probing into a single coordinator process that exposes health results and system-wide state over HTTP, eliminating split-brain between consumers and removing bind-mount mismatches between host and container `.health/` directories.

## Architecture
- **Coordinator daemon**: `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — Express server on port **3034**; LaunchAgent plist; probes services on 30s cycle; maps results to sub-check keys consumed by frontend
- **Cheap semantic LLM probe**: sends `"say OK"` / validates with `/ok/i` regex — ~1s round-trip; `semantic_ok=true` on match
- **Strong semantic probe**: `pollProxySemanticStrong()` runs in parallel with the cheap probe on the same 30s cycle; sends a substantive LLM reasoning query; result exposed as `semantic_strong_ok` boolean in `/health/state`; the coordinator is the single authority — consumers must not probe LLM semantics independently
- **Three-state semantic signal**: `semantic_ok` (cheap) + `semantic_strong_ok` (strong) together let the statusline render ✅ / `[🧠⚠️]` / ❌
- **Real-traffic gate (updated)**: previously relied on `knowledge_pipeline.lastObservationAt` (lags 10–15s due to the observation-writer's own internal LLM call). Now queries the proxy's `/api/token-usage/recent?process=<observation-writer>` endpoint, which records call timestamps **synchronously on LLM completion** — accurate, no lag. The `process` query parameter enables targeted per-process filtering via an in-memory filter in `server.mjs`
- **Network/proxy state**: polls `location`, `proxy_running`, `proxy_functional`, `internet_reachable` on 30s interval; valid network states: `N:OPEN`, `N:CN`, `N:VPN`
- **Latency-based CN vs VPN heuristic**: ping latency to a CN-internal host — <5ms = `N:CN`, 20–100ms+ = `N:VPN`
- **Proxy auto-enable logic**: when `N:CN` detected and port 3128 is listening but proxy env vars absent, sets them automatically
- **`proxy_running` logic**: requires both env vars set AND port 3128 listening
- **DNS staleness**: long-running coordinator processes accumulate stale DNS resolver state — restart periodically
- **Registry file**: tracks coordinator PID and `startTime`; stale entries cause "degraded" status (correct behavior); uptime display inflates for stale entries
- **`overallStatus` computation**: includes proxy/network health
- **obs_api auto-heal FSM**: triggers restart on "unreachable" status via `restartObsApi` in `health-remediation-actions.js`
- **ETM safety-net auto-spawn**: coordinator detects ETM not running and auto-respawns
- **LevelDB/Qdrant sub-check mapping**: probe results map to legacy sub-check keys; restart coordinator to force re-probe
- **`toUiStatus` mapping**: handles `'passed'` as a recognised status value
- **LSL time window**: `/health/state` exposes `lsl_meta.current_window` via `getTimeWindow()` from `timezone-utils.js`

## Key Files
- `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — coordinator daemon; real-traffic gate (queries proxy token-usage); `pollProxySemanticStrong()`; network/proxy state; `N:OPEN`/`N:CN`/`N:VPN`; latency heuristic; proxy auto-enable; `toUiStatus`
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — `/api/token-usage/recent` with `process` query filter (in-memory)
- `scripts/timezone-utils.js` — `getTimeWindow()` / `utcToLocalTime()`
- `config/health-verification-rules.json` — rules; hostnames must be `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`; `bind_mount_freshness` category deleted
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — reverse-proxies to coordinator; forwards `network` object
- `scripts/health-remediation-actions.js` — includes `restartObsApi`
- LaunchAgent plist — loads coordinator on host; `bootout`/`bootstrap` cycle after env var changes

## Usage
Runs as a LaunchAgent on the host. All health consumers call it via HTTP on port 3034. Network/proxy state is authoritative — no consumer should detect network mode independently. Both `semantic_ok` and `semantic_strong_ok` must be read by the statusline. **Restart the coordinator periodically** to prevent DNS resolver staleness.

## Troubleshooting
- **Real-traffic gate fires too early / lags 10–15s**: Old code used `lastObservationAt`; verify the gate queries `/api/token-usage/recent?process=<observation-writer>` and that `process` filter is active in proxy `server.mjs`
- **`[🧠⚠️]` never appears**: Verify `semantic_strong_ok` in `/health/state`; confirm `pollProxySemanticStrong()` is on the poll cycle
- **Location reports `N:OPEN` on corporate network**: Stale DNS resolver state — restart coordinator immediately
- **Proxy not auto-enabled on corporate network**: Verify port 3128 is listening and both env vars are absent
- **Dashboard shows "degraded" coordinator status**: Stale PID in registry file — clear the stale entry
- **Sub-checks show "unknown"**: Coordinator has not completed first 30s probe cycle — restart to force re-probe
- **`network` field always `undefined` on frontend**: `handleGetHealthStatus` in `server.js` not passing the `network` object explicitly
- **Stale env vars after plist change**: Run `launchctl bootout` then `launchctl bootstrap`

entities.name: LLM CLI Proxy — VPN/Corporate Network Detection
observations:
## Purpose
Provides a local HTTP proxy bridge that routes LLM completion requests to one of five providers (claude-code, Copilot, Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic) based on network context, optional process-level override pins, and a multi-tier fallback ladder that handles OAuth rate limits and auth failures distinctly.

## Architecture
**Provider priority order**:
- **Home/public network**: `claude-code → copilot → groq → openai → anthropic`
- **Corporate/VPN network**: `copilot → claude-code → groq → openai → anthropic`

**Claude-code OAuth fallback ladder** (in priority order):
1. **Direct OAuth bearer** — `completeClaudeCodeDirect()` uses bearer token from macOS keychain; fastest path, Max subscription billing
2. **CLI/spawn path** — `claude --print` subprocess; lands in a different Anthropic rate-limit bucket (~2.9s on Sonnet via CLI); succeeds when direct OAuth returns 429
3. **Copilot cross-provider** — last resort only; invoked when both claude-code paths exhausted

**Rate-limit vs auth error distinction**: bridge distinguishes HTTP 429 (rate-limit → try CLI/spawn) from 4xx auth errors (credentials issue → fail immediately).

**CRITICAL — env-key sanitization**: if `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is present in the environment when calling any claude-code path, Anthropic bills the API key prepay balance instead of the Max OAuth subscription. Key must be absent for OAuth-path calls to use correct billing tier. Lesson saved to `memory/feedback_claude_cli_env_sanitize.md`.

**`/api/token-usage/recent` endpoint**: accepts an optional `process` query parameter; filtered **in-memory** on the server side to return only records for the specified process. Used by the health coordinator's real-traffic gate to determine the synchronous last-call timestamp for the observation-writer process without the 10–15s lag from `lastObservationAt`.

**Network mode detection**: `bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` queries health coordinator's `/health/state` at startup — eliminates split-brain where startup and statusline reported different modes.

**User hash and timezone injection**: `start-llm-proxy.sh` computes and exports before spawning the bridge:
- `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` — via env-var-driven dynamic import of `user-hash-generator.js`
- `LSL_TIMEZONE` — placed between the final `fi` block and the `exec node` log line

**Process-level hard pin** (Stage-0 override): unconditionally replaces `body.provider` and `body.model` even when the caller explicitly sends a provider; falls through only if the pinned provider is unreachable.

**CORS**: `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` must include `PUT`; missing `PUT` blocks all preflight requests while `GET` succeeds silently.

**Settings routes**: `server.mjs` exposes `GET /settings` and `PUT /settings` for user-facing model configuration.

## Key Files
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — routing, OAuth fallback ladder, `completeClaudeCodeDirect()`, OAuth keychain helpers, 429 vs auth error distinction, CORS, Stage-0 override, `GET`/`PUT` settings routes, `/api/token-usage/recent` with `process` filter
- `rapid-llm-proxy/bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` — startup; queries `/health/state`; computes `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH`; exports `LSL_TIMEZONE`
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/user-hash-generator.js` — user hash computation; imported dynamically to avoid stdout pollution
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/token-usage.ts` — hourly bucketing for token usage reporting
- `memory/feedback_claude_cli_env_sanitize.md` — env sanitization lesson for claude-code OAuth calls

## Usage
Runs as a background launchd service. Network mode detected once at startup. The OAuth fallback ladder activates automatically on 429 responses. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` must not be present in the environment for claude-code calls to use the Max subscription billing path. The `/api/token-usage/recent?process=<name>` endpoint is queried by the health coordinator for the real-traffic gate. Restart via `launchctl` after changes.

## Troubleshooting
- **Sonnet calls returning `[Raw]` / 429 errors**: CLI/spawn path should activate automatically — verify 429 distinction logic in `server.mjs`
- **Billing hitting API key prepay instead of Max subscription**: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` present in environment — remove it; see `memory/feedback_claude_cli_env_sanitize.md`
- **`/api/token-usage/recent?process=` returning all records**: `process` filter not active in `server.mjs` — verify in-memory filter applies when `process` query param is present
- **Health coordinator real-traffic gate still lags 10–15s**: Old code path using `lastObservationAt`; verify coordinator queries proxy token-usage endpoint instead
- **`PUT /settings` fails with CORS preflight error**: `PUT` missing from `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` in `server.mjs`
- **`LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` contains multi-line garbage**: Using `import(process.argv[1])` — verify env-var-driven `user-hash-generator.js` dynamic import pattern
- **Statusline shows home but startup detected corporate (split-brain)**: `start-llm-proxy.sh` not querying `/health/state` — verify it does not run its own `curl --proxy` probe

entities.name: Health Verification Rules — Bind Mount Freshness and Container Stability
observations:
## Purpose
Defines the rules used by the health verifier (and, post-Phase-33, the health coordinator) to assess system health. Misconfigured rules — especially `bind_mount_freshness` entries referencing files no longer bind-mounted — cause containers to be force-recreated in an infinite loop.

## Architecture
- **File**: `config/health-verification-rules.json` — consumed by `health-verifier.js` (pre-Phase-33) and directly by `scripts/health-coordinator.js` (post-Phase-33)
- **`bind_mount_freshness` rule**: checks that specific bind-mounted files exist inside the container and match expected sizes; if a file is removed from the bind-mount but remains in the rule list, every health check fails permanently, triggering the auto-heal recreate loop
- **`bind_mount_freshness` rule deleted**: as part of Phase 33, this rule category was removed from the rules engine entirely in favour of Docker healthcheck for container-process supervision
- **Port targets**: rules check port **3031** for the constraint dashboard API (not 3030, which is the host-only Next.js UI)
- **Container health check target**: `localhost:8080` (host-side)

## Key Files
- `config/health-verification-rules.json` — authoritative rule list; must be kept in sync with actual bind-mount configuration
- `scripts/health-coordinator.js` — reads rules directly post-Phase-33
- `config/health-verifier.js` — pre-Phase-33 consumer

## Usage
Rules are evaluated on every health probe cycle (30s). Container recreate is triggered by the auto-heal mechanism when violations are detected. After any change to Docker bind-mounts, audit `bind_mount_freshness.files` entries and remove references to unmounted files.

## Troubleshooting
- **Container recreating every ~5 minutes in a loop**: A `bind_mount_freshness.files` entry references a file no longer bind-mounted (e.g., `scripts/consolidate-observations.js` after the obs-api cutover) — remove the stale entry from `health-verification-rules.json` and restart the container
- **Health check always failing for a specific sub-check**: Verify the rule's port/path target is correct and that the referenced service is actually running at that address inside the container
- **Rules not taking effect after edit**: Post-Phase-33, coordinator reads the file directly — restart the coordinator process; pre-Phase-33, restart the container

entities.name: Health Monitor Log Rotation and Stale File Cleanup
observations:
## Purpose
Prevents unbounded log growth and stale health data from abandoned projects from polluting the health monitoring system. Without rotation, logs can grow to several gigabytes; without cleanup, stale `.health/` JSON files cause false "DOWN" alerts for projects no longer running.

## Architecture
- **Log rotation**: `health-verifier.js` and `statusline-health-monitor.js` both include a 10 MB rotation limit added to prevent runaway log growth
- **Capture file cleanup**: `tmux-session-wrapper.sh` includes a 14-day retention policy for tmux capture files
- **Stale health files**: `.health/` JSON files are written by running projects; files older than ~3 days from stopped projects should be removed manually — no automatic eviction exists for the `.health/` directory itself
- **False alarm mechanism**: the LSL health status hook reads `.health/*.json` files and defaults to `DOWN` on JSON parse errors or missing data; stale files from dead projects cause persistent false red indicators in the statusline

## Key Files
- `scripts/health-verifier.js` — 10 MB log rotation added
- `scripts/statusline-health-monitor.js` — 10 MB log rotation added
- `scripts/tmux-session-wrapper.sh` — 14-day capture file cleanup
- `.health/` directory — runtime health JSON files; must be manually audited for staleness

## Usage
Log rotation is passive and automatic once configured. Stale `.health/` file cleanup requires periodic manual intervention (or a cron/launchd job). When a project is decommissioned, delete its corresponding `.health/` JSON file to prevent false alerts.

## Troubleshooting
- **LSL shows red / "DOWN" for a project that is not running**: Stale `.health/` JSON file exists for a stopped project — delete the file; also check for JSON parse errors in the health hook
- **Logs consuming several GB of disk**: Rotation was not configured; truncate existing oversized logs manually and verify the 10 MB rotation limit is active in `health-verifier.js` and `statusline-health-monitor.js`
- **Dashboard "all green" but LSL red**: The two systems read different health sources — dashboard may use incomplete rules while the LSL hook reads `.health/` JSON files directly; reconcile by auditing both rule sets and cleaning stale files

entities.name: System Health Dashboard — server.js API Backend
observations:
## Purpose
Serves health check data, observations, digests, insights, and consolidation status to the dashboard frontend on port 3032. Acts as the bridge between host-side health sources (coordinator, SQLite, Memgraph) and the browser UI.

## Architecture
- **Port**: 3033 (inside Docker container); frontend at port 3032 consumes it
- **Route order**: `/api/*` endpoints registered before `express.static` and SPA catch-all in `static-server.js`; wrong order causes API calls to return HTML
- **`/report` response schema**: must include `summary`, `checks` (array of ≥23 items), and `recommendations: []` — frontend store silently discards the entire payload if `summary` is absent; frontend crashes on `.recommendations.length` if the field is missing
- **Port Liveness panel**: filters checks by `check.category === 'services'`; the `category` field must be present in the port check mapping or the panel shows all ports as DOWN
- **Timestamp fields**: violation and system-check rows must include a `.timestamp` field; omitting it causes `RangeError: Invalid time value` in the frontend table components
- **Status translation**: server maps internal statuses to `"passed"`/`"failed"`/`"warning"` before sending to frontend
- **Memgraph freshness**: `handleGetCGRFreshness` uses Node's built-in `net` module (not `nc`/netcat) to check port 7687 connectivity — netcat is unavailable in the container environment
- **Consolidation `stderrAgeMs`**: server computes and forwards `stderrAgeMs` (from `lastStderrAt`) in the inflight consolidation status object for staleness detection
- **SSE handler**: stat-gates the 618 KB JSON read on `mtimeMs + size`; uses adaptive intervals (100 ms with active clients, 1 s idle) to minimise CPU

## Key Files
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js` — all API routes, response shaping, Memgraph check, SSE handler, consolidation status
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/static-server.js` — route ordering: `/api/*` proxy before static/catch-all
- `docker/docker-compose.yml` — bind-mount for dashboard static assets

## Usage
Runs inside the `coding-services` Docker container. Frontend (port 3032) fetches from this server. Health data is proxied from the host coordinator at `HEALTH_COORDINATOR_URL`. Rebuild the Next.js frontend after any `server.js` response schema changes.

## Troubleshooting
- **"No check data yet" despite coordinator returning checks**: `summary` field missing from `/report` response — add it to align with frontend parser expectations
- **Port Liveness panel shows all DOWN**: `category: 'services'` field absent from port check mapping — add it to the mapping in `server.js`
- **Frontend crashes on render**: `recommendations` field missing — add `recommendations: []` to response
- **`RangeError: Invalid time value` in tables**: `.timestamp` field missing from violation/system-check rows — add timestamps to each row emitted by server
- **Memgraph freshness always false-negative**: `nc` was used for port check; must use Node `net` module instead
- **API calls return HTML**: `/api/*` route mounted after `express.static` — fix route order in `static-server.js`

entities.name: Combined Status Line — tmux Session Health Display
observations:
## Purpose
Renders per-session health indicators, project labels, lifecycle icons, and global health status in the tmux statusline, pulling live data from the health coordinator and active transcript files across all supported coding agents (Claude, OpenCode).

## Architecture
- **Health data source**: `getGlobalHealthStatus()` fetches from the coordinator HTTP endpoint (port 3034); stale `.health/*.json` reads removed
- **Multi-agent transcript scanning**: `transcriptAgeMs()` in `csl.js` / `status-line-fast.cjs` iterates **all** sessions for a project (not just the first match) and picks the **freshest** transcript mtime across both Claude `.jsonl` files and OpenCode `.specstory/history/` markdown files; taking the minimum age ensures dormant sessions don't override active ones
- **Sleep/wake heartbeat guard**: ETM heartbeat promotion only overrides transcript-derived status when the transcript is moderately stale (≥45-minute gate); prevents post-sleep wake events from falsely promoting idle projects to 🟢
- **Graduated lifecycle icons**: maps transcript age to lifecycle bucket — `🟢→🟡→🟠→🟤→⚫→💤`; Unicode 13+ glyphs replaced to avoid `wcwidth` mismatches
- **`[📚]` online learning badge**: when a project is cooling down (no recent LSL input), the badge renders bubble-lifecycle colors (🟠→🟤→⚫→💤) using the existing `lsl[*].transcriptPath` mtime signal; yellow/red are reserved exclusively for genuine pipeline impairment during **active** sessions; `unreachable` overrides to yellow regardless
- **Three-state semantic health indicator**: consumes `semantic_strong_ok` from `/health/state` — ✅ (both cheap + strong probes pass), `[🧠⚠️]` amber (cheap probe passes, strong probe fails), ❌ (both probes fail); prevents false-green signals when shallow LLM probing succeeds but deeper reasoning is degraded
- **`'corporate'` network mode**: `combined-status-line.js` must accept `'corporate'` as a healthy proxy network mode
- **Emoji cell-width — tmux config fix**: `visibleCellWidth()` handles "Ambiguous" width codepoints; tmux/xterm.js mismatch fixed via `~/.tmux.conf`: `set -g codepoint-widths "U+26A0=2"`
- **LSL fallback**: `getLSLHealthStatus()` includes a project-level fallback when `TMUX_PANE` is absent
- **D-11 lock mechanism**: prevents concurrent statusline updates

## Key Files
- `scripts/combined-status-line.js` — all statusline logic; coordinator fetch, per-pane labels, lifecycle icons, `visibleCellWidth()`, LSL fallback, `'corporate'` mode check, D-11 lock, `[📚]` badge lifecycle colors, `[🧠⚠️]` amber semantic branch
- `scripts/status-line-fast.cjs` — fast variant; `transcriptAgeMs()` with multi-agent freshness (`.jsonl` + `.specstory/history/`) and heartbeat gate; updated in parallel with main script
- `~/.tmux.conf` — must contain `set -g codepoint-widths "U+26A0=2"` for correct ⚠️ width accounting in VS Code terminal
- `docs-content/guides/status-line.md` — reference documentation

## Usage
Invoked by tmux's `status-right`/`status-left` hooks on a timer. Multiple parallel tmux sessions each render pane-specific labels while sharing coordinator-sourced global health data. Both Claude and OpenCode sessions are automatically detected as activity sources. The `[📚]` badge and `[🧠⚠️]` semantic badge appear alongside project labels when relevant.

## Troubleshooting
- **Project shows 🟢 after laptop sleep/wake despite long inactivity**: Heartbeat fired on wake without the 45-minute staleness gate — verify gate is present in `status-line-fast.cjs` heartbeat promotion logic
- **Active OpenCode session shows 💤**: `transcriptAgeMs()` not scanning `.specstory/history/` markdown files — verify specstory path is included in the multi-source accumulation loop; both `.jsonl` and specstory paths must be checked with no early exit on Claude-only hits
- **Multiple sessions showing stale status**: `transcriptAgeMs()` using `.find()` (first match) instead of iterating all sessions for minimum age — verify it iterates all sessions and picks the freshest mtime
- **`[📚]` badge shows yellow for inactive project**: Badge should use bubble-lifecycle colors (🟠→🟤→⚫→💤) during cooldown, not yellow; yellow is reserved for active-session impairment — verify the cooldown branch checks transcript mtime via `lsl[*].transcriptPath`
- **`[🧠⚠️]` badge never appears despite semantic probe degradation**: Verify the statusline reads `semantic_strong_ok` from the coordinator `/health/state` response and that the amber branch is implemented before the ✅ branch in the rendering chain
- **Residue characters / trailing digits in statusline (VS Code only)**: tmux/xterm.js wcwidth mismatch — verify `~/.tmux.conf` contains `set -g codepoint-widths "U+26A0=2"`; reload with `tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf`
- **Do NOT strip emojis to fix width**: the correct fix is the `~/.tmux.conf` codepoint-widths directive
- **Proxy network mode shows error despite healthy corporate connection**: `'corporate'` not in the valid-mode check — add it alongside `'vpn'`/`'public'` in `combined-status-line.js`

entities.name: GitHub Submodule Push — SSH vs HTTPS Authentication
observations:
- The project has multiple submodules: `code-graph-rah`, `mcp-server-semantic-analysis`, `constraint-monitor`, `mcp-constraint-monitor`, and the main repo
- **SSH key ambiguity**: the SSH config resolves to a QCOM work account (`Frank-Woernle-<COMPANY>-de_QCOM`) instead of the personal account (`fwornle`) for some remotes; root cause is in `~/.ssh/config`, not in OpenCode config
- **Workaround**: switch affected remotes from SSH to HTTPS (`git remote set-url origin https://github.com/...`) for submodules where the work SSH key is incorrectly selected
- `code-graph-rah` was already up-to-date; `mcp-server-semantic-analysis` and the main repo pushed successfully via HTTPS
- `constraint-monitor` and `mcp-constraint-monitor` failed with SSH access denied as of 2026-05-14; no fix was applied
- The underlying fix requires auditing `~/.ssh/config` host entries to ensure personal account key is selected for `github.com` personal repos

## Troubleshooting
- **SSH push fails with "Permission denied (publickey)"**: Check `ssh -T git@github.com` to see which account is resolved; if it shows the QCOM account, use HTTPS remotes as a workaround
- **HTTPS push requires credentials**: Use a GitHub personal access token stored in the macOS keychain or `~/.netrc`
- **Permanent fix**: Audit `~/.ssh/config` — add a `Host github.com-personal` entry with the correct identity file and update affected remote URLs to use that host alias

entities.name: LLM Token Usage Tracking — Proxy Instrumentation and Dashboard
observations:
## Purpose
`rapid-llm-proxy`'s token usage module records per-process LLM token consumption in hourly bucketed JSON files so the system health dashboard can visualise token spend without requiring centralised aggregation.

## Architecture
- **`src/token-usage.ts`** — core tracking module:
  - `resolveTokenExportDir()` — export directory path; overridden by `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR`
  - `currentWindow()` — computes hourly time window string using `LSL_TIMEZONE`
  - `lslWritePath()` — constructs full path for current hour's JSON file, incorporating `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH`
  - `exportToHourFile()` — writes/appends token usage; SELECT and INSERT OR IGNORE both include `model_raw`; files missing `model_raw` fall back to `model` for backward compatibility
  - Per-window-keyed debounce `Map` — throttles high-frequency writes within the same hour
- **Startup wrapper** (`bin/start-llm-proxy.sh`) — sets `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` to `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/`; also exports `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` and `LSL_TIMEZONE` before bridge spawn
- **Export layout**: per-hour JSON files in `year-month/` subdirectories; filename includes `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH`
- **Schema**: export rows include `model_raw` (raw provider model string) alongside canonicalized `model`

**Process attribution (`body.process` field):**
- Callers must include `body.process` in their HTTP requests to the proxy; the proxy records this as the `process` column in `token-usage.db`
- Wave-analysis HTTP calls currently omit `body.process` → all wave-analysis rows logged as `process='unknown'`
- Phase 52 adds `body.process` to wave-analysis HTTP calls
- The health coordinator's real-traffic gate uses `/api/token-usage/recent?process=<observation-writer>` to query per-process timestamps; `process` filter is applied in-memory in `server.mjs`

**Dashboard routing label (cosmetic vs. actual):**
- `token-usage.tsx` hardcodes `"Groq: llama-3.3-70b-versatile"` as the display name for the "standard" tier in dashboard constants
- This is a **cosmetic label only** — it does not reflect actual runtime routing
- Actual provider preference order: `claude-code → copilot → groq` (groq is the last-resort fallback; confirmed zero Groq calls in live `token-usage.db` against all `claude-code` and `copilot` traffic)
- Phase 52 (pending): fix dashboard constants to reflect actual routing order

**Dashboard visualisation** (`src/pages/token-usage.tsx`) — Recharts-based view with treemap:
- **Known issue**: treemap has no `Tooltip` (smaller boxes non-interactive)
- **Known issue**: 8 model name spelling variants from 3 uncanonicalized callsites in `server.mjs`
- **Treemap 50:50 split artifact**: occurs in 24h view when pre-fix calls coexist — switch to "Last 1h" to see accurate post-fix data

**LLM routing settings dialog** (`src/pages/token-usage-settings-dialog.tsx`) — flex-column layout with overflow-scrollable middle region; header and footer remain pinned while the table scrolls independently.

**Plan E status** (input / cache_read / cache_create 3-column split): **not yet implemented**; currently the dashboard merges all token types into a single column.

**PlantUML diagrams** (`docs/diagrams/`): three in-place diagrams — `component-model.puml`, `request-flow.puml`, `architecture-overview.puml`.

## Key Files
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/token-usage.ts` — hourly bucketing; `resolveTokenExportDir`, `currentWindow`, `lslWritePath`, `exportToHourFile`, per-window debounce `Map`; `model_raw` in SELECT and INSERT OR IGNORE
- `rapid-llm-proxy/bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` — sets `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR`; exports `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` and `LSL_TIMEZONE`
- `coding/scripts/user-hash-generator.js` — hash computation; imported dynamically by `start-llm-proxy.sh`
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — routes completions, records token counts; 3 uncanonicalized model-name callsites; `process` in-memory filter for `/api/token-usage/recent`
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/token-usage.tsx` — dashboard visualisation; hardcoded `"Groq: llama-3.3-70b-versatile"` label (cosmetic); treemap missing `Tooltip`
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/token-usage-settings-dialog.tsx` — LLM routing settings dialog; flex-column layout with pinned header/footer and scrollable middle
- `rapid-llm-proxy/docs/diagrams/` — architecture PlantUML sources; updated in-place

## Usage
`token-usage.ts` runs inside the bridge process. `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH`, `LSL_TIMEZONE`, and `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` must be present in the bridge environment — all three injected by `start-llm-proxy.sh`. Export files accumulate under `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/<year-month>/`. All LLM HTTP callers must include `body.process` in their requests for accurate attribution. Restart via `launchctl` after changes to `start-llm-proxy.sh` or `token-usage.ts` (TypeScript must be rebuilt first).

## Troubleshooting
- **Dashboard shows "Groq: llama-3.3-70b-versatile" as primary provider**: This is a hardcoded cosmetic label in `token-usage.tsx` dashboard constants — not the actual routing; real traffic uses `claude-code → copilot → groq` order; Phase 52 fixes the label
- **Wave-analysis or UKB calls show `process='unknown'` in token-usage.db**: `body.process` omitted from HTTP requests to the proxy — Phase 52 adds this field to wave-analysis calls; verify all LLM service callers include the `process` field
- **Health coordinator real-traffic gate lags 10–15s**: Old code path using `lastObservationAt`; verify coordinator queries `/api/token-usage/recent?process=<observation-writer>` and that `process` in-memory filter is active in `server.mjs`
- **Model names show inconsistent spellings in token usage view**: 3 uncanonicalized callsites in `server.mjs` emit raw strings — implement `canonicalizeModelName()` at each log call site
- **Treemap boxes not interactive / no tooltip**: No `Tooltip` component in treemap — add `Tooltip` to `token-usage.tsx`
- **Treemap shows unexpected 50:50 split**: Artifact of 24h window mixing old and new data — switch to "Last 1h" filter
- **Settings dialog clips content**: Verify the flex-column overflow-scrollable layout is in place in `token-usage-settings-dialog.tsx`; pinned-header/footer + scrollable-middle is the correct pattern
- **Plan E (3-column token split) missing**: Not yet implemented — track as a pending feature
- **`LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` is a multi-line garbage string**: `start-llm-proxy.sh` using wrong import pattern — verify env-var-driven import of `user-hash-generator.js`; restart launchd after fix
- **Token export files writing to wrong location**: `LLM_PROXY_TOKEN_EXPORT_DIR` not set — verify `start-llm-proxy.sh` exports it to `.specstory/history/logs/llm-proxy-export/`
- **`model_raw` missing from exported rows**: Verify `token-usage.ts` SELECT and INSERT OR IGNORE both include `model_raw`; legacy files without it should fall back to `model` in hydration logic

entities.name: Observation and Digest Retention — SQLite Pruning and JSON Cold-Store
observations:
## Purpose
Caps the SQLite `observations` table to a 7-day retention window while preserving older rows in a JSON cold-store, with the observations API transparently merging both sources for any query range so the dashboard shows a continuous history regardless of pruning.

## Architecture
Five components delivered across Phase 35 waves:

- **ObservationWriter retention floor** (35-01): enforces a minimum retention boundary on writes, preventing pruning of freshly-ingested observations; tested in `ObservationWriter.retention-floor.test.js`
- **ObservationPruner** (35-02): 91-line nightly job removes rows from `observations` beyond the 7-day boundary; `insights` table is permanently excluded from all DELETE statements; 5-test Jest suite
- **ColdStoreReader** (35-03): reads JSON cold-store files and maps them to the same row shape as SQLite results; 7-test Jest suite
- **Obs-API wiring** (35-04): pure helpers extracted into `scripts/observations-api-merge.mjs` (avoids a Jest/TS-dist chain breakage); back-compat re-exports maintained in `observations-api-server.mjs`; Set-based dedup logic carries load-bearing inline comments at both call sites; handler gate removed from `observations-api-server.mjs`
- **Full-union pagination** (35-07): fetches the entire SQLite in-range slice without LIMIT/OFFSET, unions with cold-store rows in-memory, deduplicates, sorts by `timestamp DESC`, then slices per page — replaces the former "offset=0-only-cold" contract where SQLite rows saturated all page slots and hid cold rows entirely

**Pagination total fix** (35-06): `_mergeObservations` previously reported `total = sqliteTotal + coldTotal`, overcounting; now reports the true merged-and-deduplicated count.

**Live data volumes**: ~1,855 total rows (712 SQLite + 1,143 cold) for the April 1–May 15 range; cold rows first appear on pages 15+ and are marked with a ❄ snowflake icon in the UI; `coldOnFirstPageOnly=false` confirmed via live probe.

**Known boot race**: if the pruner executes while the consolidator is mid-write during `SIGTERM`, the pruner may report `"database disk image is malformed"` — `PRAGMA integrity_check` confirms the DB is actually healthy; fix is a second obs-api restart after the WAL checkpoints.

**Known anomaly**: `_origin: null` appears on SQLite rows at `offset=200`; cold-store rows correctly carry `_origin: 'cold'` — flagged for follow-up.

## Key Files
- `src/live-logging/ObservationPruner.js` — nightly pruning; excludes `insights` table
- `src/live-logging/ColdStoreReader.js` — JSON cold-store reader; maps to SQLite row shape
- `scripts/observations-api-merge.mjs` — merge helper; full-union pagination; Set-based dedup
- `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` — API server; handler gate removed; delegates to merge helper
- `tests/scripts/observations-api-server.merge.test.js` — 38-test suite for merge logic
- `.planning/phases/35-observation-digest-retention-with-json-cold-store-fallback/` — per-wave PLAN and SUMMARY files

## Usage
Pruner runs nightly. To surface cold-store rows in the dashboard UI, set the date picker to a range older than 7 days — cold rows appear interleaved with SQLite rows, distinguished by the ❄ icon. The merge API is transparent to all consumers of `/api/observations`. Verify Phase 35 gate with: `curl 'http://localhost:12436/api/observations?limit=10'` and check for `fromColdStore: true`.

## Troubleshooting
- **Dashboard shows "No observations yet" on last pages**: Pagination total overcounting — verify `_mergeObservations` reports the true merged count, not `sqliteTotal + coldTotal`
- **Cold rows not visible despite date range spanning retention boundary**: Full-union pagination must be active — verify `observations-api-server.mjs` does not apply LIMIT/OFFSET to SQLite before the cold-store union; confirm handler gate removal is deployed
- **`"database disk image is malformed"` at pruner boot**: Boot race between consolidator SIGTERM and pruner execution — run `PRAGMA integrity_check` to confirm DB health, then restart obs-api to allow WAL checkpoint before retrying
- **Cold rows always zero / `fromColdStore: false`**: `ColdStoreReader.js` not wired into the API handler — verify Phase 35-04 wiring is deployed
- **`_origin: null` on some SQLite rows**: Known anomaly at offset=200; cold-store rows carry `_origin: 'cold'`; SQLite rows have no `_origin` set — no action required yet

entities.name: LLM Proxy Bridge (rapid-llm-proxy) — Provider Routing, Network Detection, and Settings
observations:
## Purpose
Provides a local HTTP proxy bridge that routes LLM completion requests to one of five providers (claude-code, Copilot, Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic) based on network context and optional process-level override pins. Distinct from the TypeScript `integrations/llm-cli-proxy` service.

## Architecture
**Provider priority order**:
- **Home/public network**: `claude-code → copilot → groq → openai → anthropic`
- **Corporate/VPN network**: `copilot → claude-code → groq → openai → anthropic`

**Network mode detection**: `bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` queries the health coordinator's `/health/state` endpoint for network mode (e.g., `[N:HOME] [P:OFF]`) at startup, rather than running its own `curl --proxy` probe. This eliminates the split-brain state where the startup script and the statusline reported different modes.

**Provider registration**: env-gated `init` blocks in `server.mjs` register `openai` and `groq` as available when their API keys are present; `start-llm-proxy.sh` loads the relevant API key env vars at startup.

**Dispatcher**: a `switch` statement in `server.mjs` routes to `completeOpenAI` and `completeGroq` functions (previously existed but were unreachable due to missing branches — now wired).

**Process-level hard pin** (Stage-0 override): when an override pin is active, it must unconditionally replace `body.provider` and `body.model`, even when the caller (e.g., `health-coordinator`) explicitly sends `body.provider='copilot'`. The pin falls through to the caller's value only if the pinned provider is unreachable. This fix was designed and partially applied in `server.mjs`.

**CORS**: `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` in `server.mjs` must include `PUT`. A missing `PUT` entry blocks all `PUT` preflight requests while `GET` succeeds silently.

**Settings routes**: `server.mjs` exposes `GET /settings` and `PUT /settings` for user-facing model configuration; a Stage-0 provider override check is included in the auto-router.

## Key Files
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — routing, dispatcher switch, CORS headers (must include `PUT`), process-level override logic, `GET`/`PUT` settings routes
- `rapid-llm-proxy/bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` — startup; queries health coordinator `/health/state`; loads API key env vars; env-gated provider init blocks
- `_work/rapid-llm-proxy/src/token-usage.ts` — hourly bucketing for token usage reporting

## Usage
Runs as a background service (via launchd). Network mode is detected once at startup by querying the health coordinator. All LLM completion requests from agents not using the TypeScript `llm-cli-proxy` flow through this bridge. Restart via `launchctl` after changes to `server.mjs`.

## Troubleshooting
- **All requests routed to Copilot regardless of provider**: Process-level override pin is bypassed by a caller-supplied `body.provider` — the override must be unconditional when a pin is active; verify Stage-0 logic in `server.mjs`
- **`PUT /settings` fails with CORS preflight error**: `PUT` missing from `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` in `server.mjs` — add it to the CORS header list and restart via `launchctl`
- **Statusline shows home but startup detected corporate (split-brain)**: Startup script was running its own `curl --proxy` probe — verify `start-llm-proxy.sh` now queries `/health/state` from the health coordinator instead
- **Groq/OpenAI calls silently routed to Copilot despite keys present**: Dispatcher `switch` missing branches for `completeOpenAI`/`completeGroq`, or env-gated init blocks not executed — verify both branches exist and API key env vars are exported before bridge spawn
- **Bridge starts with wrong network mode**: `start-llm-proxy.sh` failed to reach the health coordinator at startup — check coordinator is running on port 3034 before the bridge launches

entities.name: Observations API Server — Host-Side HTTP Wrapper and Process Lifecycle
observations:
## Purpose
`observations-api-server.mjs` is a standalone host-side HTTP service (port 12436) that replaces direct SQLite access from the Docker-containerized dashboard layer. It merges SQLite and cold-store observations and serves them via HTTP, following the established host-API wrapper pattern used for OKB/VKB.

## Architecture
- **Port**: 12436 (host-side); the dashboard backend (port 3033) reverse-proxies `/api/observations` to this service
- **Merge helper**: pure pagination and dedup helpers live in `scripts/observations-api-merge.mjs`, extracted from `observations-api-server.mjs` to avoid a Jest/TS-dist chain issue; back-compat re-exports are preserved in `observations-api-server.mjs`; Set-based dedup carries load-bearing inline comments at both call sites
- **Full-union pagination**: fetches all SQLite in-range rows (no LIMIT/OFFSET), unions with cold-store rows via `ColdStoreReader`, deduplicates, sorts by `timestamp DESC`, then slices per page — supports queries spanning the 7-day retention boundary transparently
- **Phase 35 gate**: confirmed operational — probing `http://localhost:12436/api/observations?limit=10` returns `fromColdStore: true`, 10 SQLite rows + 862+ cold rows with correct shape
- **Consolidation route**: `/api/consolidation/run` is proxied through this service; a 502 on this endpoint at port 3033 is a reliable indicator that obs-api is down
- **Process lifecycle**: managed by a LaunchAgent; when killed with signal -9 (common during triage), port 3033 has no upstream and returns 502 for all proxied requests; restart via `launchctl kickstart -k` targeting the obs-api LaunchAgent
- **SIGTERM hardening** (pending implementation): two gaps for orphaned CLI child processes spawned by the server:
  1. Abort signal propagation to in-flight `fetch` calls
  2. Explicit kill of spawned CLI subprocesses on client disconnect — without this, orphaned `claude` CLI processes accumulate after client disconnects

## Key Files
- `scripts/observations-api-server.mjs` — primary API server; full-union handler; handler gate removed (Phase 35-04)
- `scripts/observations-api-merge.mjs` — pure merge/pagination helpers; Set-based dedup; back-compat re-exports
- `src/live-logging/ColdStoreReader.js` — cold-store reader called by merge helper
- `tests/scripts/observations-api-server.merge.test.js` — 38-test merge suite
- LaunchAgent plist — manages obs-api lifecycle on host

## Usage
Runs as a LaunchAgent on the host. Restart after code changes via `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.coding.obs-api` (adjust service name as needed). Verify the gate: `curl 'http://localhost:12436/api/observations?limit=10'` and check for `fromColdStore: true`.

## Troubleshooting
- **502 on port 3033 dashboard** (including `/api/consolidation/run`): obs-api (port 12436) is down — check for signal-9 kill in logs, restart via launchctl; also look for an orphaned `claude` CLI process holding resources
- **`"database disk image is malformed"` at boot**: Boot race — consolidator was mid-write during `SIGTERM`; run `PRAGMA integrity_check` to confirm DB is healthy, then restart obs-api to allow WAL checkpoint before retrying
- **Orphaned `claude` CLI processes accumulating**: obs-api spawns CLI subprocesses not reaped on disconnect — pending SIGTERM hardening: add abort signal propagation to `fetch` calls and explicit subprocess kill on client disconnect in `server.mjs`
- **Cold rows missing from API response / `fromColdStore: false`**: ColdStoreReader not wired — verify Phase 35-04 handler gate removal is deployed and `observations-api-merge.mjs` is imported
- **Back-compat import errors**: helpers moved to `observations-api-merge.mjs`; verify back-compat re-exports exist in `observations-api-server.mjs` for any consumer importing directly from the server file

entities.name: rapid-llm-proxy Standalone Package
observations:
## Purpose
`@rapid/llm-proxy` is a domain-agnostic LLM abstraction layer shared across the OKB and coding projects. It normalizes multiple LLM providers behind a single interface, handles corporate proxy routing, tracks per-process token usage, and manages startup environment injection for downstream consumers.

## Architecture
- **Proxy bridge** (`proxy-bridge/server.mjs`): routes LLM completions across up to five providers with network-aware priority ordering; exposes `GET /settings` and `PUT /settings` for model configuration; includes Stage-0 process-level provider override
- **Startup wrapper** (`bin/start-llm-proxy.sh`): queries health coordinator `/health/state` for network mode; loads API key env vars; computes and exports two critical env vars before spawning the bridge:
  - `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` — deterministic short hash (e.g., `c197ef`) computed via env-var-driven dynamic import of `user-hash-generator.js` (the dynamic-import pattern prevents stdout contamination that caused the earlier multi-line garbage value bug)
  - `LSL_TIMEZONE` — user's local timezone (e.g., `Europe/Berlin`), exported between the final `fi` block and the `exec node` log line
- **Token usage tracking** (`src/token-usage.ts`): hourly `strftime`-based bucketing; export output path `.data/llm-proxy-export/*.json` is git-tracked (confirmed via `.gitignore` audit); file format mirrors LSL conventions; planned migration to per-hour JSON files in `year-month/` dirs with `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` in filenames
- **Documentation** (`docs/diagrams/`): three PlantUML diagrams maintained in-place (kebab-case naming, no versioned copies):
  - `component-model.puml` — provider/token-usage components with stereotypes
  - `request-flow.puml` — per-process pin and reap steps
  - `architecture-overview.puml` — dashboard and token-usage export components

## Key Files
- `rapid-llm-proxy/proxy-bridge/server.mjs` — main bridge; routing, CORS (`PUT` required in `Access-Control-Allow-Methods`), Stage-0 override, settings routes
- `rapid-llm-proxy/bin/start-llm-proxy.sh` — startup; network mode detection; `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` via `user-hash-generator.js`; `LSL_TIMEZONE` export
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/user-hash-generator.js` — hash computation module; imported dynamically in startup script
- `rapid-llm-proxy/src/token-usage.ts` — hourly token usage bucketing; export to `.data/llm-proxy-export/*.json`
- `rapid-llm-proxy/docs/diagrams/component-model.puml` — component model diagram
- `rapid-llm-proxy/docs/diagrams/request-flow.puml` — request flow diagram
- `rapid-llm-proxy/docs/diagrams/architecture-overview.puml` — architecture overview

## Usage
Runs as a launchd background service. Network mode is detected once at startup from the health coordinator (port 3034). `LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` and `LSL_TIMEZONE` are available to the bridge and to any downstream consumer that needs user-scoped or timezone-aware file naming. Restart via `launchctl` after changes to `server.mjs` or `start-llm-proxy.sh`. PlantUML diagrams are updated in-place and PNGs regenerated after architecture changes.

## Troubleshooting
- **`LLM_PROXY_USER_HASH` is a multi-line garbage string**: Caused by using `import(process.argv[1])` which triggered `runIfMain()` and polluted stdout — revert `start-llm-proxy.sh` via `git checkout`, restart launchd, verify the env-var-driven `user-hash-generator.js` import pattern is in place
- **`LSL_TIMEZONE` missing from bridge environment**: Check `start-llm-proxy.sh` export is placed between the final `fi` and the `exec node` log line; verify `launchctl` restarted after the change
- **`PUT /settings` blocked by CORS preflight**: `PUT` missing from `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` in `server.mjs` — add and restart
- **Bridge starts with wrong network mode**: Health coordinator not running on port 3034 at startup time — ensure coordinator is up before the bridge launches
- **Token usage export file growing unboundedly**: Migration to per-hour JSON files in `year-month/` directories is planned but not yet implemented — monitor `.data/llm-proxy-export/` file size manually in the interim
- **PlantUML diagrams out of date after architecture changes**: Diagrams live in `docs/diagrams/` and must be updated in-place (no versioned copies); regenerate PNGs and commit alongside source changes

entities.name: KM-Core Foundation — v7.1 Architecture and Project Structure
observations:
## Purpose
Plan 42 migrates the UKB system's core KM subsystems (ontology, entity model, deduplication) to use km-core's shared library. Work spans a superproject/submodule split: km-core is consumed as a git submodule while UKB production code lives in the superproject. Plans 42-01, 42-03, and 42-06 are complete; Plan 42-07 (legacy deletion) is blocked at three architectural checkpoints. Phases 42.1/42.1.1/42.1.2 address UKB batch processing project-anchor parity and ontology loader bugs.

## Architecture

**Repo structure:**
- `src/ontology/` symlink resolves to the superproject; agent/tools files live in the submodule — split commits required for changes spanning both
- Always import km-core via root barrel `@fwornle/km-core`; sub-path imports fail due to ESM/CJS resolution (established precedent from Plan 42-01)
- Single-plan phases under `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis` execute via sequential inline on the main worktree (no subagents) due to submodule path requirements

**OntologyRegistry adoption (Plan 42-03):**
- `LegacyOntologyAdapter` in `src/ontology/LegacyOntologyAdapter.ts` wraps km-core's `OntologyRegistry` and proxies all existing call sites
- Real km-core API names: `isValidClass` (not `hasClass`), `getAllClassNames` (not `listClasses`), `getLoadedDomains` (not `listDomains`)
- 8 ontology JSON files in flattened `.data/ontologies/` directory; km-core `OntologyFile` schema with `_legacy` field preservation; 134 classes across 8 domains
- Known callers: `tools.ts`, `persistence-agent.ts` (unexpected third caller), plus LegacyOntologyAdapter itself
- `dist/ontology/OntologyManager.js` is a stale orphaned artifact — `tsc` does not garbage-collect outputs whose sources have been deleted; manual deletion required after any source removal

**OntologyConfigManager flat-path bug (fixed in Phase 42.1.1):**
- `OntologyConfigManager.ts:126-146` expected subdirectory-structured files (e.g., `upper/upper.json`) but `.data/ontologies/` stores files flat (e.g., `upper.json`)
- Registry remained empty → `ensureProjectAnchor('Coding')` threw silently → zero `[WaveController] Project-anchor pass:` log lines in Docker logs
- SC#6 end-to-end verifier returned FAIL: 68 orphans out of 106 in-scope entities
- Phase 42.1.1 fix: flat-path resolver added to the loader; barrel re-export updated

**Missing `Project` class (Phase 42.1.2, pending):**
- `Project` ontology class absent from `.data/ontologies/upper.json` → `ensureProjectAnchor('Coding')` throws even after the loader flat-path fix
- Phase 42.1.2: add `Project` class to `upper.json`; fix `getStatus()` to return `upperOntologyPath: string | undefined` with guarded callers in `tools.ts`

**UKB batch path project-anchor parity (Phase 42.1):**
- Standard processing path has `_ensureProjectAnchor`; UKB batch path (`ukb-workflow`) lacks an equivalent → 64 double-orphaned entities with zero project linkage edges
- Phase 42.1 (`ukb-project-anchor-parity`) adds parity between standard and batch paths
- `ukb-workflow-modal.tsx` is an artifact edited during Phase 42.1 execution

**Canonical entity emission — known field omissions:**
- `canonical-mapper.ts` emits entity shapes without `team` and `project` fields
- `km-core-adapter.ts` silently discards the `team` parameter downstream
- Both gaps allow entity shapes to pass through the pipeline without team/project linkage, compounding orphan counts

**LLMService process identifier:**
- Wave-analysis HTTP calls omit `body.process` → `process='unknown'` in `token-usage.db` rows for all UKB/wave-analysis invocations
- Phase 52 covers this fix alongside dashboard routing label corrections

**Canonical entity emission (Plan 42-06):**
- `augmentWithCanonical` helper in `src/canonical-mapper.ts` merges canonical fields onto the existing `KGEntity` shape, preserving all legacy fields for downstream consumers
- `runId` added to `WaveController.execute()` in `src/wave-controller.ts` and threaded into Wave1/2/3 constructors
- `src/wave1-project-agent.ts` and `src/wave2-component-agent.ts` call `toCanonicalEntity`/`augmentWithCanonical` at the final return point (after all enrichment)
- `createOntologySystem` signature changed to 3-argument form; call sites in `tools.ts` and `persistence-agent.ts` updated; property renamed `.manager` → `.ontology`

**Dedup rewire (Plan 42-06):**
- `mergeEntityGroup` (a non-mutating log-only stub) fully deleted from `src/deduplication.ts`
- Replaced by `mergeDuplicateGroup` helper wrapping km-core's `mergeEntities`; gracefully skips when no store reference is available

**Entity migration script (Plan 42-03):**
- `src/migration/migrate-ontologies.ts` handles ontology flattening per Plan 42-03 conventions using `.data/ontologies` path patterns
- Must run against a safe DB copy — `GraphDatabaseService` holds the LevelDB lock
- Live results: 802 entities migrated, `errorCount: 0`; 19 entities (2.4%) flagged `ontologyClassUnregistered` for `Project`/`System`/`Knowledge`
- Migrated store lives at `.data/knowledge-graph-migrated/`

**Architectural blockers — Plan 42-07 (no code changes made):**
1. `GraphDatabaseService.js` is live — imported by `graph-database-adapter.ts`; cannot be deleted without rewiring the adapter
2. LevelDB layout mismatch between legacy `GraphDatabaseService` and km-core's `GraphKMStore`; recommended path (Option A): point km-core bootstrap at `.data/knowledge-graph-migrated/`
3. `QdrantSyncService.js` has live superproject consumers — cannot be deleted without first rewiring all consumers

## Key Files
- `integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/OntologyConfigManager.ts` — ontology loader; flat-path resolver added in Phase 42.1.1 (lines 126-146 previously broke on flat layout)
- `.data/ontologies/upper.json` — must include `Project` class after Phase 42.1.2 (currently absent)
- `src/ontology/LegacyOntologyAdapter.ts` — wraps km-core `OntologyRegistry`; proxies existing call sites
- `src/canonical-mapper.ts` — `augmentWithCanonical` helper; known gap: `team`/`project` fields not emitted
- `src/wave-controller.ts` — `runId` added to `execute()` signature
- `src/wave1-project-agent.ts` — canonical emit at final return point
- `src/wave2-component-agent.ts` — canonical emit at final return point
- `src/deduplication.ts` — `mergeDuplicateGroup` wrapping km-core's `mergeEntities`; stub deleted
- `src/migration/migrate-ontologies.ts` — ontology flattening migration script
- `graph-database-adapter.ts` — imports `GraphDatabaseService.js` (live; blocks its deletion)
- `.data/ontologies/` — 8 flattened ontology JSON files in km-core `OntologyFile` schema
- `.data/knowledge-graph-migrated/` — migrated LevelDB store (802 entities, 0 errors)
- `dist/ontology/OntologyManager.js` — stale artifact; must be manually deleted after source removal
- `.planning/phases/42.1-ukb-project-anchor-parity/` — batch path `_ensureProjectAnchor` parity
- `.planning/phases/42.1.1-ontology-layout-resolution-.../` — flat-path loader fix
- `.planning/phases/42.1.2-.../` — `Project` class registration + `getStatus()` type fix

## Usage
- Import km-core exclusively via root barrel `@fwornle/km-core`; sub-path imports are unreliable
- Use real `OntologyRegistry` API names: `isValidClass`, `getAllClassNames`, `getLoadedDomains`
- Before running the migration script, copy the DB to a temp location to avoid LevelDB lock contention
- After any ontology source deletion, manually delete the corresponding `dist/ontology/*.js` artifact
- SC#6 end-to-end verifier is the acceptance gate for project-anchor correctness; all three phases (42.1, 42.1.1, 42.1.2) must be deployed before SC#6 is expected to PASS

## Troubleshooting
- **SC#6 verifier FAIL (68 orphans out of 106 entities)**: Three cascading causes — (1) `OntologyConfigManager.ts` flat-path bug (fixed in 42.1.1), (2) `Project` class missing from `upper.json` (Phase 42.1.2), (3) UKB batch path missing `_ensureProjectAnchor` (Phase 42.1); deploy all three before re-running SC#6
- **Zero `[WaveController] Project-anchor pass:` lines in Docker logs since a given timestamp**: OntologyConfigManager registry is empty due to the flat-path loader bug — verify Phase 42.1.1 flat-path resolver is deployed and the container restarted
- **`ensureProjectAnchor('Coding')` throws silently**: Two possible causes — (1) loader flat-path bug (verify 42.1.1 deployed), (2) `Project` class absent from `upper.json` (verify 42.1.2 deployed)
- **`process='unknown'` in `token-usage.db` rows for wave-analysis calls**: `body.process` omitted from wave-analysis HTTP requests; Phase 52 adds this field
- **Orphaned entities with zero project linkage edges in UKB batch run**: UKB batch path lacks `_ensureProjectAnchor` equivalent — Phase 42.1 adds parity; check `ukb-workflow-modal.tsx` edits
- **`canonical-mapper.ts` output entities missing `team`/`project` fields**: Known gap — `km-core-adapter.ts` silently discards team parameter; both files require explicit field propagation
- **ESM/CJS sub-path import fails at runtime**: Use root barrel `@fwornle/km-core`
- **`OntologyManager` substring found by grep**: Check `dist/ontology/OntologyManager.js` — stale artifact; `tsc` does not GC deleted-source outputs
- **Migration script fails / LevelDB locked**: `GraphDatabaseService` holds the lock — copy the DB to a temp location before running `migrate-ontologies.ts`
- **`GraphDatabaseService.js` deletion causes build errors**: It is imported by `graph-database-adapter.ts` — must rewire the adapter first
- **Wave agents missing canonical fields on output entities**: Verify `toCanonicalEntity`/`augmentWithCanonical` are called at the **final** return point in each wave agent (after all enrichment)
- **`createOntologySystem` call site errors**: Signature is 3-argument; property name is `.ontology` (not `.manager`)

entities.name: GSD Workflow Conventions — Command Naming and Skill Invocation
observations:
## Purpose
Establishes the canonical format for GSD slash-commands and workflow skill invocations used by the orchestrator agent and surfaced to the developer, preventing the recurring mistake of using deprecated colon syntax, and documenting behavioral rules for each major workflow mode.

## Architecture
- **Correct format**: `/gsd-command` (hyphen separator) — e.g., `/gsd-execute-phase`, `/gsd-plan-phase`, `/gsd-code-review`
- **Deprecated format**: `/gsd:command` (colon separator) — must not be used or surfaced in suggestions, workflow file references, or agent output
- **Memory file (authoritative)**: `feedback_gsd_command_nomenclature.md` in the Claude projects memory directory; supersedes a removed duplicate `feedback_gsd_skill_naming.md`
- **Workflow file hygiene**: any colon-form references found inside workflow definition files must be translated to hyphen-form before surfacing them to the user

**Workflow execution patterns** (GSD SDK):

| Workflow | Key Behavior |
|---|---|
| `gsd-plan-phase --gaps` | Gap-closure mode: skips research, reads `VERIFICATION.md` directly, validates existing artifacts (`VALIDATION.md`, `PATTERNS.md`, `VERIFICATION.md`, `REVIEW.md`, existing plans), checks UI/schema gates, queues pattern-mapper then planner |
| `gsd-execute-phase --gaps-only` | Filters plans with `gap_closure: true` in frontmatter, then proceeds with standard wave-based subagent spawning |
| `gsd-execute-phase` (standard) | Wave-based parallel sub-agent orchestration with dependency analysis; single-plan waves are normal (no-parallelism result is expected for sequential repos like km-core) |
| `gsd-code-review` | Dispatches to `gsd-code-reviewer` agent; `--fix` triggers `gsd-code-fixer` for Critical and Warning findings (Info excluded unless `--all` is also passed) |
| `gsd-code-review` (external repo) | Path-validation gate rejects cross-repo absolute paths sourced from `SUMMARY.md` when files live in an external repo (e.g., `km-core`); workaround: directly spawn `gsd-code-reviewer` with absolute paths to bypass the gate |
| `add-phase` | Appends a new phase to the current milestone in `ROADMAP.md`; if the phase already exists, execution pauses and asks the user to clarify before writing |
| `discuss-phase` | Routes to `discuss-phase.md` in "discuss" mode; loads context from `REQUIREMENTS.md` and prior phase `CONTEXT.md` to avoid re-litigating settled decisions; produces `CONTEXT.md` for the new phase |
| `gsd-cleanup` | Archives completed milestone phase directories into `.planning/milestones/v{X.Y}-phases/`; produces a dry-run summary for user confirmation before performing any destructive moves |

**Gate resolution in `gsd-plan-phase`**: checks for existing `CONTEXT.md` (skip discuss-phase if present), `research` config flag, UI/AI-SPEC/ORM indicators, then queues pattern-mapper before planner.

## Key Files
- `memory/feedback_gsd_command_nomenclature.md` — canonical memory file; single source of truth for hyphen-form convention (duplicate `feedback_gsd_skill_naming.md` removed)
- `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — target of `add-phase` and `gsd-cleanup` workflows; phase entries at named line positions
- GSD workflow definition files — must use `/gsd-command` format internally; audit before surfacing to user

## Usage
The agent applies this convention at all times when suggesting, generating, or describing GSD skill invocations. When loading workflow files that contain colon-form references, translate to hyphen-form before output. Use `--gaps` / `--gaps-only` for targeted gap-closure runs without re-executing already-complete plans. Run `discuss-phase` before `plan-phase` for any new phase without an existing `CONTEXT.md`; `gsd-cleanup` after milestone completion to archive phase directories.

## Troubleshooting
- **Agent suggests `/gsd:execute-phase`**: Colon format — correct to `/gsd-execute-phase`; update any workflow file containing the colon form
- **Duplicate memory files for same convention**: Consolidate to `feedback_gsd_command_nomenclature.md`; remove duplicates
- **`gsd-code-review --fix` only fixes some findings**: By design — `--fix` targets Critical and Warning only; pass `--all` to include Info findings
- **`gsd-code-review` fails with path-validation error on external repo files**: Files live outside the current repo's tree; bypass the gate by directly spawning `gsd-code-reviewer` with absolute paths
- **`add-phase` stalls without writing**: Phase already exists in `ROADMAP.md` — inspect the conflict line number and decide whether to skip, overwrite, or adjust the existing entry before re-running
- **`gsd-plan-phase --gaps` still runs full research**: Confirm `--gaps` flag is parsed before the research step; check that `VERIFICATION.md` is present for the coordinator to read
- **`gsd-cleanup` moves directories without confirmation**: Dry-run summary should be presented first; if it was skipped, check whether the session ended during the scan phase before any writes occurred

entities.name: UKB Integration — km-core Adoption (Plan 42)
observations:
## Purpose
Plan 42 migrates the UKB system's core KM subsystems (ontology, entity model, deduplication) to use km-core's shared library. Work spans a superproject/submodule split: km-core is consumed as a git submodule while UKB production code lives in the superproject. Plans 42-01, 42-03, and 42-06 are complete; Plan 42-07 (legacy deletion) is blocked at three architectural checkpoints.

## Architecture
**Repo structure:**
- `src/ontology/` symlink resolves to the superproject; agent/tools files live in the submodule — split commits required for changes spanning both
- Always import km-core via root barrel `@fwornle/km-core`; sub-path imports fail due to ESM/CJS resolution (established precedent from Plan 42-01)

**OntologyRegistry adoption (Plan 42-03):**
- `LegacyOntologyAdapter` in `src/ontology/LegacyOntologyAdapter.ts` wraps km-core's `OntologyRegistry` and proxies all existing call sites
- Real km-core API names differ from plan-text aliases: `isValidClass` (not `hasClass`), `getAllClassNames` (not `listClasses`), `getLoadedDomains` (not `listDomains`)
- 8 ontology JSON files in flattened `.data/ontologies/` directory; km-core `OntologyFile` schema with `_legacy` field preservation; 134 classes across 8 domains
- Known callers: `tools.ts`, `persistence-agent.ts` (unexpected third caller, discovered during migration), plus LegacyOntologyAdapter itself
- All source occurrences of the substring `OntologyManager` eliminated from `src/` (including comments, field names, and utility files); grep acceptance criterion satisfied
- `dist/ontology/OntologyManager.js` is a stale orphaned artifact — `tsc` does not garbage-collect outputs whose sources have been deleted; manual deletion required after any source removal

**Canonical entity emission (Plan 42-06):**
- `augmentWithCanonical` helper in `src/canonical-mapper.ts` merges canonical fields onto the existing `KGEntity` shape, preserving all legacy fields (`type`, `level`, `parentId`, etc.) for downstream consumers like `mapEntityToSharedMemory`
- `runId` added to `WaveController.execute()` in `src/wave-controller.ts` and threaded into Wave1/2/3 constructors
- `src/wave1-project-agent.ts` and `src/wave2-component-agent.ts` call `toCanonicalEntity`/`augmentWithCanonical` at the final return point (after all enrichment)
- `createOntologySystem` signature changed to 3-argument form; call sites in `tools.ts` and `persistence-agent.ts` updated; property renamed `.manager` → `.ontology`

**Dedup rewire (Plan 42-06):**
- `mergeEntityGroup` (a non-mutating log-only stub) fully deleted from `src/deduplication.ts`
- Replaced by `mergeDuplicateGroup` helper wrapping km-core's `mergeEntities`; gracefully skips when no store reference is available

**Entity migration script (Plan 42-03):**
- `src/migration/migrate-ontologies.ts` handles ontology flattening per Plan 42-03 conventions using `.data/ontologies` path patterns
- Must run against a safe DB copy — `GraphDatabaseService` holds the LevelDB lock; copy DB to a temp location first
- Live results: 802 entities migrated, `errorCount: 0`; 19 entities (2.4%) flagged `ontologyClassUnregistered` for `Project`/`System`/`Knowledge` (within 5% budget)
- Migrated store lives at `.data/knowledge-graph-migrated/`

**Architectural blockers — Plan 42-07 (no code changes made):**
1. **`GraphDatabaseService.js` is live** — imported by `graph-database-adapter.ts`; cannot be deleted without rewiring the adapter
2. **LevelDB layout mismatch** — legacy `GraphDatabaseService` and km-core's `GraphKMStore` use different LevelDB layouts; atomic dir-swap blocked; recommended path (Option A, smallest blast radius): point km-core bootstrap at `.data/knowledge-graph-migrated/`, leave legacy store untouched
3. **`QdrantSyncService.js` has live superproject consumers** — cannot be deleted without first rewiring all consumers

## Key Files
- `src/ontology/LegacyOntologyAdapter.ts` — wraps km-core `OntologyRegistry`; proxies existing call sites
- `src/ontology/registry-adoption.test.ts` — TDD adoption tests
- `src/canonical-mapper.ts` — `augmentWithCanonical` helper; preserves legacy `KGEntity` fields
- `src/wave-controller.ts` — `runId` added to `execute()` signature
- `src/wave1-project-agent.ts` — canonical emit at final return point
- `src/wave2-component-agent.ts` — canonical emit at final return point
- `src/deduplication.ts` — `mergeDuplicateGroup` wrapping km-core's `mergeEntities`; `mergeEntityGroup` stub deleted
- `src/migration/migrate-ontologies.ts` — ontology flattening migration script
- `src/migration/migrate-ontologies.test.ts` — migration TDD test suite
- `graph-database-adapter.ts` — imports `GraphDatabaseService.js` (live; blocks its deletion)
- `.data/ontologies/` — 8 flattened ontology JSON files in km-core `OntologyFile` schema
- `.data/knowledge-graph-migrated/` — migrated LevelDB store (802 entities, 0 errors)
- `dist/ontology/OntologyManager.js` — stale artifact; must be manually deleted after source removal

## Usage
- Import km-core exclusively via root barrel `@fwornle/km-core`; sub-path imports are unreliable
- Use real `OntologyRegistry` API names: `isValidClass`, `getAllClassNames`, `getLoadedDomains`
- Before running the migration script, copy the DB to a temp location to avoid LevelDB lock contention with the running `GraphDatabaseService`
- After any ontology source deletion, manually delete the corresponding `dist/ontology/*.js` artifact — `tsc` will not GC it
- For Plan 42-07 unblocking: get a user decision on which option to take for each of the 3 checkpoints before touching any files

## Troubleshooting
- **ESM/CJS sub-path import fails at runtime**: Use root barrel `@fwornle/km-core` — sub-path imports are unreliable per Plan 42-01 precedent
- **`OntologyManager` substring found by bare-substring grep**: Check `dist/ontology/OntologyManager.js` — stale artifact must be manually deleted; `tsc` does not GC deleted-source outputs
- **Migration script fails / LevelDB locked**: `GraphDatabaseService` holds the lock — copy the DB to a temp location before running `migrate-ontologies.ts`
- **`ontologyClassUnregistered` warnings for `Project`/`System`/`Knowledge`**: These classes are intentionally absent from the 8 ontology files; 2.4% rate is within the 5% acceptance budget
- **`GraphDatabaseService.js` deletion causes build errors**: It is imported by `graph-database-adapter.ts` — must rewire the adapter first (Option 2) or defer deletion (Option 1/3) before removing the file
- **`QdrantSyncService.js` deletion causes build errors**: Live superproject consumers exist — identify and rewire all consumers before attempting deletion
- **LevelDB atomic dir-swap blocked**: Layout mismatch between legacy and km-core stores; use Option A: point km-core bootstrap at `.data/knowledge-graph-migrated/` and leave the legacy store untouched
- **Wave agents missing canonical fields on output entities**: Verify `toCanonicalEntity`/`augmentWithCanonical` are called at the **final** return point in each wave agent (after all enrichment, not before)
- **`createOntologySystem` call site errors**: Signature is 3-argument; property name is `.ontology` (not `.manager`); update both `tools.ts` and `persistence-agent.ts`

entities.name: SessionWindowManager
observations: SessionWindowManager assigns LSL session entries to hourly time-window buckets (e.g., '0800-0900') that are embedded directly in LSL metadata, meaning the window label becomes part of the persisted file identity rather than a query-time annotation

File routing decisions are made by SessionWindowManager at write time, so sessions that span an hourly boundary would require explicit handling to avoid being silently truncated or duplicated across two window files

The time-window format implies wall-clock hour alignment rather than session-duration alignment, meaning a session starting at 08:59 and ending at 09:10 crosses a window boundary that SessionWindowManager must resolve

SessionWindowManager interacts with the LSLConfigValidator indirectly, as RedactionEngine's validated config is applied before persistence — implying SessionWindowManager consumes already-redacted content, not raw transcripts

Because TranscriptAdapter.watchTranscripts() in lib/agent-api/transcript-api.js resets lastEntryCount only on instantiation, SessionWindowManager must tolerate replayed entries on process restart without creating duplicate window-file segments

The hourly segmentation pattern suggests SessionWindowManager uses the session's start timestamp (from getCurrentSession() output) rather than individual entry timestamps to assign the window, keeping all entries from one session in a single file

entities.name: RedactionEngine
